headings: h3: Contact details

pages.text_excerpt: About Stephanie - Stephanie Peacock - Stephanie Peacock Skip to main content Stephanie Peacock The official website of Stephanie Peacock, the Member of Parliament for Barnsley South Join Get Involved Donate About About Stephanie News Barnsley Chronicle Column Social Media Parliament Contact Email Stephanie Email Barnsley Council Privacy Policy Office Standards More Menu About Stephanie Stephanie Peacock MP decorating with poppies ahead of Remembrance Day 2022 Stephanie was elected to serve as the Member of Parliament for Barnsley East in the 2017 General Election. She was re-elected in December 2019, and returned to Parliament with an increased majority to represent Barnsley South in 2024. Following Labour’s election victory she was asked by the Prime Minister to serve in his Government as a Minister in the Department of Culture, Media and Sport. Stephanie was the Parliamentary Chair of Hope not Hate, an anti fascist campaign organisation, Chair of the Industrial Heritage All-Party Parliamentary Group, and Co-Chair of the All-Party Parliamentary Group for Veterans. She has successfully campaigned to increase the maximum sentences for those convicted of causing death by dangerous driving . She led the campaign for justice for retired miners, calling for an end to the Government taking money from miners’ pensions which totals over £4 billion to date. She has held Parliamentary debates, lobbied the previous Government, and successfully secured a Parliamentary Inquiry into the issue, which called for significant reform. She has completed two of the Parliamentary Armed Forces Schemes with the Army and the Royal Airforce and in 2022 graduated as a member of the Royal College of Defence Studies. Stephanie studied History at Queen Mary University of London, and received a Master’s degree from University College London. A qualified teacher, she taught History in a secondary school before working in adult education across Yorkshire. Stephanie then spent four years as a Political Officer for the GMB trade union. An active member of the Labour Party for over twenty years, Stephanie served for four years on the party’s Governing body, the National Executive Committee, and for a decade on the party’s policy making body, the National Policy Forum. She was a member of the Opposition Whips Office, Shadow Cabinet Office Minister, Shadow Environment Minister, and a Shadow Defence Minister. Stephanie is a Patron of BIADS, a local Barnsley charity helping people with dementia. A member of the Women’s Parliamentary Football Team and a keen runner, she completed the London Marathon in 2019. Stephanie lives in Barnsley. Stephanie Peacock Contact details Email: stephanie.peacock.mp@parliament.uk Further details Promoted by Karen Rowling on behalf of Stephanie Peacock PO Box 825 Barnsley S73 3DU ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved.
